facade_colour: warm red-brown brick, slightly darker than 122

hcd_data.statement_of_contribution: Each property has value as a nineteenth-century House-form building that reflects vernacular interpretations of Victorian architectural styles. Through its construction date and typology, each property is directly associated with the District’s early residential development from the 1850s to the early 1900s. Constructed as a semi-detached pair, the properties retain most of their original building features including their mirrored facades, shared roofline with central gable, first storey bay windows, brick cladding with polychromatic brickwork, and fenestration pattern with segmental openings. Contextually, each property shares a consistent setback, scale, and massing with the other contributing properties on the street. Each property supports and maintains the residential character of the streetscape and is functionally, visually, and historically linked to the District.

context.street_character: One-way | Residential street | Tree-lined | Heritage character

photo_observations.facade_colour_observed: warm red-brown brick with buff/cream polychromatic accents, metal balcony railings added
photo_observations.facade_condition_notes: Brick in fair condition; some modern alterations to windows and balcony
photo_observations.window_details: Replacement windows with dark frames; segmental arched openings retained; upper gable window
photo_observations.door_details: Central entrance with modern glass storm door
photo_observations.bay_window_details: First-floor projecting bay window, mirrored pair with 122 Bellevue. Canted three-sided bay with segmental-arched openings.
photo_observations.porch_details: Concrete stoop with metal railings at entrance.
photo_observations.decorative_details: Brick voussoirs above windows; decorative brick patterns
photo_observations.alterations_visible: Metal balcony railings added at upper floors. Black-painted metal fence at front.
photo_observations.notes: Semi-detached pair with 122; photo at oblique angle. Yard with scattered debris.

notes: Semi-detached pair with 122; photo at oblique angle. Yard with scattered debris.

facade_detail.composition: The street elevation spans roughly 5.2 metres across 3 storeys and is capped by a gable, consistent with its house-form, semi-detached typology.
facade_detail.opening_rhythm: The facade keeps a domestic opening rhythm of 2, 2, 1 window bays from lower to upper levels, using mixed, and 1 principal entrance.
facade_detail.heritage_expression: Character-defining facade elements include decorative brick, original windows, bay window, cornice.
facade_detail.heritage_summary: Constructed as a semi-detached pair, the properties retain most of their original building features including their mirrored facades, shared roofline with central gable, first storey bay windows, brick cladding with polychromatic brickwork, and fenestration pattern with segmental openings. Contextually, each property shares a consistent setback, scale, and massing with the other contributing properties on the street.

facade_description: Semi-detached three-storey house-form in warm red-brown brick, slightly darker than 122 with a gable roof. Features include a ground-floor canted and decorative bargeboards. Metal balcony railings added at upper floors. Black-painted metal fence at front. Pre-1889 construction.
heritage_summary: Constructed as a semi-detached pair, the properties retain most of their original building features including their mirrored facades, shared roofline with central gable, first storey bay windows, brick cladding with polychromatic brickwork, and fenestration pattern with segmental openings. Contextually, each property shares a consistent setback, scale, and massing with the other contributing properties on the street.
